title: botrychium virginianum subsp. typicum
intro: Botrychium virginianum subsp. typicum, also known as the Virginia grapefern, is a subspecies of the fern family that is native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Botrychium virginianum subsp. typicum include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and treating various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea or by using the roots and leaves in a tincture.
